messages.content:
- What was observed:
  - High input error counters on GigabitEthernet0/0/1 on R1 and R2 (R1: 57, R2: 59) while both interfaces remained up.

- Diagnosis (main cause):
  - Speed/duplex negotiation mismatch: R2 was forced to 100 Mbps half-duplex with auto-negotiation disabled while R1 was using auto-negotiation — causing link-layer errors.

- Changes made:
  - Enabled auto-negotiation on router2 Gi0/0/1 (running-config now shows negotiation auto). Interface already administratively up.

- Verification:
  - Result: FAILED (partial). The configuration change was applied (negotiation=auto on R2), but no post-change interface status was collected.
  - Remaining tasks: collect post-change interface status ("show interface") for Gi0/0/1 on both R2 and R1 (duplex, speed, input_errors, last_change) to confirm both ends re‑negotiated to the same speed/duplex and that input_errors have cleared.
